front: What is the timeline and majority required to approve a National Emergency (Article 352)?
back: Within 1 month by a Special Majority in both Houses.

front: What are the three grounds for declaring a National Emergency?
back: War, External Aggression, and Armed Rebellion.

front: Which amendment replaced 'Internal Disturbance' with 'Armed Rebellion' for National Emergency?
What is the timeline and majority required to approve President's Rule (Article 356)?
back: Within 2 months by a Simple Majority in both Houses.

front: What is the timeline and majority required to approve Financial Emergency (Article 360)?
back: Within 2 months by a Simple Majority in both Houses.

front: Which two fundamental rights can NEVER be suspended during any emergency?
Which article provides for the automatic suspension of Article 19 during war or external aggression?
Which article empowers the President to suspend the enforcement of other Fundamental Rights (except 20 & 21) during emergencies?
What is the maximum duration President's Rule can be extended (with parliamentary approvals every 6 months)?
Which amendment is known as the 'Mini-Constitution'?
Which amendment converted the Right to Property into a legal right under Article 300A?
Which amendment introduced the Anti-Defection Law (Tenth Schedule)?
Which amendment lowered the voting age from 21 to 18 years?
Which amendment gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions (Part IX)?
Which amendment gave constitutional status to Municipalities (Part IX-A)?
Which amendment made elementary education a Fundamental Right (Article 21A)?
Which amendment capped the size of the Council of Ministers at 15% of the Lok Sabha / State Assembly strength?
Which amendment introduced the Goods and Services Tax (GST) and the GST Council (Article 279A)?
Which amendment introduced the 10% EWS reservation in education and employment?
In which landmark case was the Basic Structure Doctrine propounded by the Supreme Court?
